message:
feat(abi): port Itanium __cxa_guard_acquire/release/abort trio (bd-3xclg)

Add the Itanium C++ ABI static-local-initialization guard primitives.
The compiler emits these around every function-scope `static` variable
initializer in C++; missing them breaks linking of any non-trivial C++
binary.

Per the Itanium ABI, the guard is a 64-bit value where byte 0 is the
"fully initialized" flag and byte 1 is the "in-progress" flag. The
trio:

  - __cxa_guard_acquire(*mut u64) -> c_int — returns 1 to the unique
    winner that should run the initializer (waiting if another thread
    is currently initializing); returns 0 to threads arriving after
    initialization completes.
  - __cxa_guard_release(*mut u64) — marks initialized and wakes
    waiters.
  - __cxa_guard_abort(*mut u64) — clears the in-progress bit without
    setting initialized (called when the initializer threw).

Concurrency uses a single global Mutex<()> + Condvar serializing all
guard transitions. Static initialization is not a hot path, so the
global serialization is acceptable and avoids the complexity of
per-guard futexes.

NULL guard pointers are defended-against (acquire returns 0, the
others are no-ops) rather than dereferenced.

Tests: 5 abi tests covering first-acquire-wins, release+re-acquire,
abort-allows-re-race, NULL no-op, and an 8-thread concurrent race
that asserts exactly one winner and seven losers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3v3","title":"Harness: asupersync deterministic orchestration for conformance + runtime_math scenarios","description":"Integrate /dp/asupersync into glibc-rs-harness runner.

Scope:
- Deterministic scheduling, traceability ids, structured results.
- Orchestrate scenario runs that exercise runtime_math controllers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Harness runner uses asupersync primitives for reproducible runs and evidence logging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:42.149982753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:21:29.149784585Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:21:29.149765789Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1022,"issue_id":"bd-3v3","author":"CobaltForge","text":"Implemented asupersync-backed deterministic conformance orchestration in glibc-rs-harness.

- Added `glibc_rs_harness::asupersync_orchestrator` (feature `asupersync-tooling`) to run fixture verification deterministically (stable fixture-set + case ordering), assign stable `trace_id`s, and emit structured evidence via `asupersync_conformance::{SuiteResult, TestResult, Checkpoint, ConformanceTestLogger}`.
- Fixed harness runner bug where fixture cases with `mode=both` were executed with mode=both (unsupported) instead of the active runner mode.
- `harness verify` now sorts fixture JSON paths deterministically and uses the orchestrator when `asupersync-tooling` is enabled; when `--report` is provided it additionally writes `<report>.suite.json` via `asupersync_conformance::write_json_report`.

Quality gates: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --all-targets` all pass.
","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:21:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vne","title":"syscall: process_vm_readv/writev all-NULL zero-count requests should succeed and preserve errno","description":"Host glibc returns success and leaves errno unchanged when process_vm_readv/process_vm_writev are called with both iov pointers NULL and both counts zero. Add focused ABI regression coverage so the full no-op fast path is frozen explicitly rather than only via one-sided zero-count cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:20:23.931045439Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:22:26.742820875Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:22:26.739757524Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity no-op process_vm_readv/process_vm_writev behavior: all-NULL zero-count requests succeed and preserve errno; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3vtx","title":"bd-1rf subtask: hosts files lookup subset implementation + explicit scope boundaries","description":"Background:
- hosts lookups are a practical dependency for many workloads and must be deterministic in files-backend scope.

Goal:
- Implement hosts files lookup subset with explicit scope boundaries and error semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Deterministic hosts parsing and lookup.
2) API mapping for supported resolver-facing calls in files scope.
3) Explicit non-goals for networked NSS backends.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Covered host lookup fixtures pass.
- Out-of-scope behavior is explicit and documented.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for IPv4/IPv6 and malformed hosts entries.
- Structured logs: trace_id, query, family, result_source, outcome, timing.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CloudyRobin","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:53.995225917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T19:03:24.895427810Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T19:02:54.796760654Z","close_reason":"Implemented and verified (hosts files subset)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","nss","resolver","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1023,"issue_id":"bd-3vtx","author":"CloudyRobin","text":"Implementation + verification evidence for `bd-3vtx`:

Implemented scope (files backend only; explicit non-goal for DNS/networked NSS fallback):
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/mod.rs`
  - added `getaddrinfo_with_hosts(...)`
  - retained numeric resolution path
  - added deterministic hosts-content lookup path and family filtering
  - explicit no-network fallback behavior (`EAI_NONAME` when unresolved in files subset)
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs`
  - wired `/etc/hosts` subset resolution in ABI `getaddrinfo` for non-numeric names
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs`
  - conformance executor supports optional `hosts_content` input for `getaddrinfo`
- `tests/conformance/fixtures/resolver.json`
  - added `getaddrinfo_hosts_file_subset` fixture

Commits carrying this work:
- `2a6d8aa` feat(resolv): add /etc/hosts file-backend lookup for getaddrinfo
- `ebb96f5` test(conformance): add hosts-file getaddrinfo resolver fixture

Verification (offloaded using `rch`, remote worker `vmi1152480`):
1. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core getaddrinfo_with_hosts -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (4 tests)
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_ipv4_lookup`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_respects_family_filter`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_case_insensitive_alias`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_no_network_fallback`
2. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance execute_getaddrinfo_case_uses_hosts_subset_when_provided -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (1 test)

Operational note: an initial `rch` run in the active shared workspace hit rsync churn due concurrent file activity by other agents and attempted local fallback; I stopped those runs and re-ran in an isolated temporary worktree to keep execution fully remote/offloaded.
","created_at":"2026-02-15T19:02:16Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3xclg","title":"PORT: __cxa_guard_acquire / __cxa_guard_release / __cxa_guard_abort (Itanium static-local init guard)","description":"Add the Itanium C++ ABI static-local-initialization guard trio: __cxa_guard_acquire(*mut u64) -> c_int; __cxa_guard_release(*mut u64); __cxa_guard_abort(*mut u64). These are emitted by the compiler around every function-scope static variable initializer in C++, so missing them breaks linking of any non-trivial C++ binary. Semantics: the guard is a 64-bit value where byte 0 holds the "fully initialized" flag and byte 1 holds the "in-progress" flag. acquire returns 1 to the unique winner that should run the initializer (waiting if another thread is currently initializing), 0 to threads arriving after init completes. release marks initialized and wakes waiters. abort releases the in-progress bit without setting initialized (called when the initializer threw). Implement with a single global Mutex+Condvar guarding all guards: simple, deadlock-free, correct under multi-thread contention; static initialization is not a hot path so the global serialization is acceptable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:00:33.100868697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:00:33.100868697Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3y2g","title":"syscall: sched_rr_get_interval negative pid with valid output should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for sched_rr_get_interval(-1, &timespec). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the negative-pid contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:26.062478207Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:51:00.697550674Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:51:00.695309401Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity sched_rr_get_interval(-1, &timespec) behavior: exact EINVAL and new ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add focused ABI regression coverage so the null-path contract for this mount API veneer is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:43:49.503182416Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:45:47.806022743Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:45:47.803834680Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity open_tree(NULL) behavior: exact EFAULT and new ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jbemm","title":"PORT: getpeereid (BSD Unix-socket peer credentials)","description":"BSD getpeereid(s, euid, egid) returns the effective uid + gid of the peer of a connected Unix-domain socket. On Linux this is a thin wrapper over getsockopt(s, SOL_SOCKET, SO_PEERCRED, &ucred, &len) — read the kernel-supplied credentials struct and split out the uid/gid fields. Returns 0 on success (writes *euid and *egid), -1 with errno on failure. Used by sshd, postfix, sudo, and other privilege-separating daemons that need to identify the local user on the other end of a socket without a userland authentication round trip. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:06:23.236695254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:10:03.712054654Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:10:03.710174520Z","close_reason":"shipped: socket_abi::getpeereid via SO_PEERCRED; 4 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jcq4","title":"syscall: get_mempolicy","description":"Add raw get_mempolicy syscall veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall veneer tests for query semantics and invalid pointer/address flag paths. Intended as the next bd-h5x raw-syscall slice; this br build rejects --parent=bd-h5x as ambiguous.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T03:26:23.410891503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T03:29:49.488009800Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T03:29:49.486025815Z","close_reason":"Added raw get_mempolicy veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall tests and passed rch core gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-jgdmx","title":"PORT: 5 Itanium C++ ABI fail-stop stubs (call_terminate / deleted_virtual / bad_cast / bad_typeid / throw_bad_array_length)","description":"Add five Itanium C++ ABI fail-stop stubs that the compiler emits at link time but that callers never expect to return. Without these symbols, every C++ binary linked against frankenlibc fails at link time. Mirror the existing __cxa_pure_virtual / __cxa_throw_bad_array_new_length pattern: print a one-line diagnostic to fd 2 and abort(). Symbols: __cxa_call_terminate(*mut c_void) -> ! (called when EH propagation hits std::terminate boundary); __cxa_deleted_virtual() -> ! (called via deleted-marked virtual function vtable slot); __cxa_bad_cast() -> ! (called when dynamic_cast<T&> fails); __cxa_bad_typeid() -> ! (called when typeid is taken on a null reference); __cxa_throw_bad_array_length() -> ! (called when array-new computes a negative length).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:53:14.605450018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:53:14.605450018Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-jgdmx","title":"PORT: 5 Itanium C++ ABI fail-stop stubs (call_terminate / deleted_virtual / bad_cast / bad_typeid / throw_bad_array_length)","description":"Add five Itanium C++ ABI fail-stop stubs that the compiler emits at link time but that callers never expect to return. Without these symbols, every C++ binary linked against frankenlibc fails at link time. Mirror the existing __cxa_pure_virtual / __cxa_throw_bad_array_new_length pattern: print a one-line diagnostic to fd 2 and abort(). Symbols: __cxa_call_terminate(*mut c_void) -> ! (called when EH propagation hits std::terminate boundary); __cxa_deleted_virtual() -> ! (called via deleted-marked virtual function vtable slot); __cxa_bad_cast() -> ! (called when dynamic_cast<T&> fails); __cxa_bad_typeid() -> ! (called when typeid is taken on a null reference); __cxa_throw_bad_array_length() -> ! (called when array-new computes a negative length).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:53:14.605450018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:56:14.936735680Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:56:14.933990084Z","close_reason":"shipped: 5 Itanium cxa fail-stop stubs (call_terminate, deleted_virtual, bad_cast, bad_typeid, throw_bad_array_length) + 5 fork-waitpid abort tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jitv","title":"dirent: execute dirent_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/dirent_ops_conformance_test.rs still exercises fixtures only through the in-process executor. Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/dirent_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity by mode, and fix any matrix-path regressions the new coverage exposes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:24:52.679711848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:00:59.658076464Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:00:59.655864305Z","close_reason":"Added dirent_ops_fixture_executes_with_host_parity_via_harness_matrix in commit c2455f7 — dispatches all fixture cases through harness conformance-matrix-case subprocess (mirrors search_ops/wide_string_ops pattern). Both in-process and subprocess paths green. All gates (cargo check/test/clippy/fmt + ubs) clean via rch + isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jm3h","title":"sysvipc: semctl IPC_RMID invalid semaphore id should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for semctl(-1, 0, IPC_RMID). Replace the broad errno-family ABI assertion with an exact host-parity contract for this variadic semctl path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:26:00.959247748Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:05:50.226710994Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:05:50.224412894Z","close_reason":"Replaced family-sentinel with exact libc::EINVAL assertion matching host glibc for semctl(-1, 0, IPC_RMID). Gates clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jmpx9","title":"[fuzz-harness] fuzz_resolv: parse_services_line assertion port > 0 too strict — parser accepts 0 per glibc","description":"Port == 0 is a valid u16 the parser accepts (matches glibc's getservent which doesn't filter port 0 either). Harness assertion 'parsed port should be positive' is wrong.","notes":"Fixed in d3184a90 — relax port>0 assertion (parser matches glibc).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:31:21.663470581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:36:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:36:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
